Module me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.core
Package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.tween

Class FlixelTween

java.lang.Object
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.tween.FlixelTween
All Implemented Interfaces:
com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Pool.Poolable
Direct Known Subclasses:
FlixelAngleTween, FlixelColorTween, FlixelFlickerTween, FlixelMotion, FlixelNumTween, FlixelPropertyTween, FlixelShakeTween, FlixelVarTween
public abstract class FlixelTween extends Object implements com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Pool.Poolable
Base class for all FlixelGDX tweens and motion interpolators.

A FlixelTween provides a flexible system for animating properties, variables, or custom behaviors over time, often used for smooth transitions, UI animations, procedural effects, and advanced gameplay motions. FlixelTweens can manipulate values such as position, color, angle, scale, or arbitrary numbers, and support features like easing, delay, repeats, ping-pong (reverse-on-repeat), pausing, and callbacks for completion events.

Common Use Cases

  • Smoothly moving sprites or objects from one position to another
  • Animating color transitions (e.g., fading to black, flashing effects)
  • Pulsing, scaling, or rotating objects for visual effects
  • Chaining tweens to create sequenced or looping animations
  • Triggering sound or code when an animation completes

How Tweens are Managed

Tweens are not updated automatically unless added to a FlixelTweenManager. By default, all tweens are managed by a single global manager, but you can create and control your own managers for local tween control (such as for a specific state or menu screen).

Each tween may be paused, resumed, finished (immediately), restarted (for repeat behavior), or canceled. Tweens are generally pooled for efficient memory use.

Extension and Implementation

Subclasses of FlixelTween implement specialized behavior:

  • FlixelPropertyTween — interpolates properties of objects using lambda getters and setters.
  • FlixelNumTween — tweens a simple numeric value using reflection.
  • FlixelColorTween — tweens between colors
  • FlixelAngleTween — smoothly rotates a value
  • FlixelLinearMotion and others — for advanced motion paths

Key Fields

  • tweenSettings: the configuration parameters for this tween (duration, repeat, easing, etc.)
  • manager: the manager that updates and contains this tween instance
  • paused, active, finished: control and status flags
  • scale: represents current tween progress, interpolated from 0 to 1
  • secondsSinceStart, executions: time-tracking for tween progress and repeats

Usage Example


 // Start a tween that moves a sprite's x value from 0 to 100 over 1 second.

 // Example with a property tween and a generic helper.
 FlixelTween tween = FlixelTween.tween(sprite, new FlixelTweenSettings()
   .addGoal(sprite::getX, 100f, sprite::setX)
   .setDuration(1f)
   .setEase(FlixelEase::cubicInOut)
   .onComplete(() -> Flixel.info("Done!"))
   .start());

 // Example with a property tween and a builder.
 FlixelTween tween = FlixelTween.tween(FlixelPropertyTween.class, FlixelPropertyTweenBuilder.class)
   .addGoal(sprite::getX, 100f, sprite::setX)
   .setDuration(1f)
   .setEase(FlixelEase::cubicInOut)
   .onComplete(() -> Flixel.info("Done!"))
   .start();

Lifecycle and Pooling

When a tween completes (naturally or by calling finish()), it is automatically released back to a pool unless flagged otherwise. Do not hold references to finished or canceled tweens if using pooling, as they may be reused. If you must keep the reference of a tween, consider taking a look at FlixelTweenType.PERSIST.

Thread Safety

Tweens and their managers are generally intended for use on the game thread only.

See Also:
  • FlixelTweenManager
  • FlixelAbstractTweenBuilder
  • FlixelTweenSettings

    Cancels this tween, removes it from its manager and automatically defaults its values.

    Cancels every active tween on the global manager.

    Completes all active non-looping tweens on the global manager.

    Snaps matching non-looping tweens to their end state in one step (large delta) and runs completion logic where applicable.

    Completes active non-looping tweens assignable to type.

  • Field Details

    • paused

      public boolean paused
      Is this tween currently paused?

    • finished

      public boolean finished
      Is this tween finished tweening?

  • Method Details

    • tween

      public static <T extends FlixelTween, B extends FlixelAbstractTweenBuilder<T, B>> B tween(Class<T> tweenType, Class<B> builderType)
      Returns a fluent builder for the given tween type.

      The tween type must be registered (e.g. via FlixelTweenManager.registerTweenType(java.lang.Class<T>, java.lang.Class<? extends me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.tween.builders.FlixelAbstractTweenBuilder<T, ?>>, java.util.function.Supplier<? extends me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.tween.builders.FlixelAbstractTweenBuilder<T, ?>>, java.util.function.Supplier<T>)); if not, then this method will throw an IllegalArgumentException.

      Pass both the tween class and its builder class so the return type is the concrete builder (B), giving full IDE support for type-specific methods (addGoal(), from(), to(), etc.) and common ones (setDuration, setEase), then FlixelTweenBuilder.start().

      Example (property): 

      
 FlixelPropertyTween tween = FlixelTween.tween(FlixelPropertyTween.class, FlixelPropertyTweenBuilder.class)
   .addGoal(sprite::getX, 100f, sprite::setX)
   .setDuration(1f)
   .start();
      Parameters:
      tweenType - The tween class (e.g. FlixelPropertyTween.class). Must be registered.
      builderType - The corresponding builder class (e.g. FlixelPropertyTweenBuilder.class).
      Returns:
      A new builder instance of type B for chaining.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - If tweenType is not registered, or the registered builder could not be instantiated.

    • tween

      public static FlixelTween tween(Object object, FlixelTweenSettings tweenSettings)
      Creates a tween with the provided settings and adds it to the global tween manager (which starts it automatically).

      If tweenSettings contains var goals (FlixelTweenSettings.addGoal(String, float)), a FlixelVarTween is created: values are applied via Flixel.reflect (property / setProperty). If it contains only property goals (getter/setter overloads of FlixelTweenSettings.addGoal(java.lang.String, float)), a FlixelPropertyTween is used. Mixing both kinds of goals on the same settings instance is not allowed.

      For fluent setup, use tween(Class, Class) with FlixelVarTweenBuilder or FlixelPropertyTweenBuilder.

      Parameters:
      object - The target (var tween root for dotted paths; property tween logical subject for FlixelPropertyTween.isTweenOf(Object, String)).
      tweenSettings - Settings including goals appropriate to the tween type.
      Returns:
      The newly created and started tween.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - If both var goals and property goals are present on tweenSettings.

    • num

      public static FlixelTween num(float from, float to, FlixelTweenSettings tweenSettings, FlixelNumTween.FlixelNumTweenUpdateCallback updateCallback)
      Creates a new numerical tween with the provided settings and adds it to the global tween manager (which starts it automatically). Shorthand for create, add and start.
      Parameters:
      from - The starting floating point value.
      to - The ending floating point value.
      tweenSettings - The settings that configure and determine how the tween should animate.
      updateCallback - Callback function for updating any variable(s) that needs the current value when the tween updates.
      Returns:
      The newly created and started tween.

    • angle

      public static FlixelTween angle(@Nullable @Nullable FlixelObject sprite, float toAngle, FlixelTweenSettings tweenSettings)
      Creates a new angle tween with the provided settings and adds it to the global tween manager.
      Parameters:
      sprite - The sprite to tween the angle of.
      toAngle - The ending angle (degrees).
      tweenSettings - The settings that configure and determine how the tween should animate.
      Returns:
      The newly created and started tween.

    • angle

      public static FlixelTween angle(@Nullable @Nullable FlixelObject sprite, float fromAngle, float toAngle, FlixelTweenSettings tweenSettings)
      Creates a new angle tween with the provided settings and adds it to the global tween manager.
      Parameters:
      sprite - The sprite to tween the angle of.
      fromAngle - The starting angle (degrees).
      toAngle - The ending angle (degrees).
      tweenSettings - The settings that configure and determine how the tween should animate.
      Returns:
      The newly created and started tween.

    • color

      public static FlixelTween color(@Nullable @Nullable FlixelSprite sprite, @Nullable @Nullable FlixelColor from, @Nullable @Nullable FlixelColor to, FlixelTweenSettings tweenSettings)
      Creates a new color tween with the provided settings and adds it to the global tween manager.

      It's advised you use this method rather than directly changing the color of a sprite, as FlixelColorTween will handle the color interpolation and apply it to the sprite smoothly, rather than causing a flash or jump in color.

      Parameters:
      sprite - The sprite to tween the color of.
      from - The starting color.
      to - The ending color.
      tweenSettings - The settings that configure and determine how the tween should animate.
      Returns:
      The newly created and started tween.

    • color

      public static FlixelTween color(@Nullable @Nullable FlixelSprite sprite, @Nullable @Nullable FlixelColor from, @Nullable @Nullable FlixelColor to, FlixelTweenSettings tweenSettings, @Nullable @Nullable Runnable onColor)
      Creates a new color tween with the provided settings and adds it to the global tween manager.

      It's advised you use this method rather than directly changing the color of a sprite, as FlixelColorTween will handle the color interpolation and apply it to the sprite smoothly, rather than causing a flash or jump in color.

      Parameters:
      sprite - The sprite to tween the color of.
      from - The starting color.
      to - The ending color.
      tweenSettings - The settings that configure and determine how the tween should animate.
      onColor - The callback to run when the tween is complete.
      Returns:
      The newly created and started tween.

    • colorRaw

      public static FlixelTween colorRaw(@Nullable @Nullable FlixelSprite sprite, @Nullable @Nullable com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color from, @Nullable @Nullable com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color to, FlixelTweenSettings tweenSettings)
      Creates a new color tween using libGDX Color values with the provided settings and adds it to the global tween manager.

      It's advised you use this method rather than directly changing the color of a sprite, as FlixelColorTween will handle the color interpolation and apply it to the sprite smoothly, rather than causing a flash or jump in color.

      Parameters:
      sprite - The sprite to tween the color of.
      from - The starting color.
      to - The ending color.
      tweenSettings - The settings that configure and determine how the tween should animate.
      Returns:
      The newly created and started tween.

    • colorRaw

      public static FlixelTween colorRaw(@Nullable @Nullable FlixelSprite sprite, @Nullable @Nullable com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color from, @Nullable @Nullable com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color to, FlixelTweenSettings tweenSettings, @Nullable @Nullable Runnable onColor)
      Creates a new color tween using libGDX Color values with the provided settings and adds it to the global tween manager.

      It's advised you use this method rather than directly changing the color of a sprite, as FlixelColorTween will handle the color interpolation and apply it to the sprite smoothly, rather than causing a flash or jump in color.

      Parameters:
      sprite - The sprite to tween the color of.
      from - The starting color.
      to - The ending color.
      tweenSettings - The settings that configure and determine how the tween should animate.
      onColor - The callback to run when the tween is complete.
      Returns:
      The newly created and started tween.

    • shake

      public static FlixelTween shake(@Nullable @Nullable FlixelSprite sprite, FlixelAxes axes, float intensity, FlixelTweenSettings tweenSettings)
      Creates a shake tween using defaults FlixelShakeTween.ShakeUnit.FRACTION and fadeOut == false (full strength each frame until the tween ends). Pooled instances are reset to those defaults before configuration.
      Parameters:
      sprite - The sprite to shake.
      axes - Axes that receive offset jitter.
      intensity - With FlixelShakeTween.ShakeUnit.FRACTION, use a small value (for example 0.05f).
      tweenSettings - Duration, ease, and callbacks.
      Returns:
      The new tween, already added to the global manager.

    • shake

      public static FlixelTween shake(@Nullable @Nullable FlixelSprite sprite, FlixelAxes axes, float intensity, FlixelTweenSettings tweenSettings, FlixelShakeTween.ShakeUnit shakeUnit, boolean fadeOut)
      Creates a shake tween with explicit FlixelShakeTween.ShakeUnit and fade-out taper.
      Parameters:
      sprite - The sprite to shake.
      axes - Axes that receive offset jitter.
      intensity - Interpretation depends on shakeUnit.
      tweenSettings - Duration, ease, and callbacks.
      shakeUnit - FlixelShakeTween.ShakeUnit.FRACTION or FlixelShakeTween.ShakeUnit.PIXELS.
      fadeOut - If true, amplitude is scaled by (1 - scale) toward the end of the tween.
      Returns:
      The new tween, already added to the global manager.

    • flicker

      public static FlixelTween flicker(@Nullable @Nullable FlixelBasic basic, FlixelTweenSettings tweenSettings)
      Creates a new flicker tween with the provided settings and adds it to the global tween manager.
      Parameters:
      basic - The basic to flicker.
      tweenSettings - The settings that configure and determine how the tween should animate.
      Returns:
      The newly created and started tween.

    • flicker

      public static FlixelTween flicker(@Nullable @Nullable FlixelBasic basic, float period, float ratio, boolean endVisibility, FlixelTweenSettings tweenSettings, @Nullable @Nullable Predicate<FlixelFlickerTween> tweenFunction)
      Creates a new flicker tween with the provided settings and adds it to the global tween manager.
      Parameters:
      basic - The basic to flicker.
      period - The period of the flicker.
      ratio - The ratio of the flicker.
      endVisibility - The visibility of the flicker at the end.
      tweenSettings - The settings that configure and determine how the tween should animate.
      tweenFunction - The function to use for the flicker.
      Returns:
      The newly created and started tween.

    • linearMotion

      public static FlixelTween linearMotion(@Nullable @Nullable FlixelObject target, float fromX, float fromY, float toX, float toY, float durationOrSpeed, boolean useDuration, FlixelTweenSettings tweenSettings)
      Creates a new linear motion tween with the provided settings and adds it to the global tween manager.
      Parameters:
      target - The target to move.
      fromX - The starting X position.
      fromY - The starting Y position.
      toX - The ending X position.
      toY - The ending Y position.
      durationOrSpeed - The duration or speed of the motion.
      useDuration - Whether to use the duration or speed.
      tweenSettings - The settings that configure and determine how the tween should animate.
      Returns:
      The newly created and started tween.

    • circularMotion

      public static FlixelTween circularMotion(@Nullable @Nullable FlixelObject target, float centerX, float centerY, float radius, float angleDeg, boolean clockwise, float durationOrSpeed, boolean useDuration, FlixelTweenSettings tweenSettings)
      Creates a new circular motion tween with the provided settings and adds it to the global tween manager.
      Parameters:
      target - The target to move.
      centerX - The center X position.
      centerY - The center Y position.
      radius - The radius of the motion.
      angleDeg - The angle of the motion.
      clockwise - The direction of the motion.
      durationOrSpeed - The duration or speed of the motion.
      useDuration - Whether to use the duration or speed.
      tweenSettings - The settings that configure and determine how the tween should animate.
      Returns:
      The newly created and started tween.

    • quadMotion

      public static FlixelTween quadMotion(@Nullable @Nullable FlixelObject target, float fromX, float fromY, float cx, float cy, float toX, float toY, float durationOrSpeed, boolean useDuration, FlixelTweenSettings tweenSettings)
      Quadratic Bézier motion (one control point). Same timing rules as linearMotion(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject, float, float, float, float, float, boolean, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.tween.settings.FlixelTweenSettings). If useDuration is true, durationOrSpeed is seconds; otherwise pixels per second along the approximated curve length.
      Parameters:
      target - The target to move.
      fromX - The starting X position.
      fromY - The starting Y position.
      cx - The control point X position.
      cy - The control point Y position.
      toX - The ending X position.
      toY - The ending Y position.
      durationOrSpeed - The duration or speed of the motion.
      useDuration - Whether to use the duration or speed.

    • cubicMotion

      public static FlixelTween cubicMotion(@Nullable @Nullable FlixelObject target, float p0x, float p0y, float p1x, float p1y, float p2x, float p2y, float p3x, float p3y, float durationOrSpeed, boolean useDuration, FlixelTweenSettings tweenSettings)
      Cubic Bézier motion. (p0x, p0y) through (p3x, p3y) with control points (p1x, p1y) and (p2x,p2y).
      Parameters:
      target - The target to move.
      p0x - The starting X position.
      p0y - The starting Y position.
      p1x - The first control point X position.
      p1y - The first control point Y position.
      p2x - The second control point X position.
      p2y - The second control point Y position.
      p3x - The ending X position.
      p3y - The ending Y position.
      durationOrSpeed - The duration or speed of the motion.
      useDuration - Whether to use the duration or speed.
      tweenSettings - The settings that configure and determine how the tween should animate.
      Returns:
      The newly created and started tween.

    • linearPath

      public static FlixelTween linearPath(@Nullable @Nullable FlixelObject target, float durationOrSpeed, boolean useDuration, FlixelTweenSettings tweenSettings, float... xy)
      Piecewise-linear path through vertices x0,y0,x1,y1,.... Requires at least two points (four floats).
      Parameters:
      target - The target to move.
      durationOrSpeed - The duration or speed of the motion.
      useDuration - Whether to use the duration or speed.
      tweenSettings - The settings that configure and determine how the tween should animate.
      xy - Alternating x and y coordinates along the path.
      Returns:
      The newly created and started tween.

    • quadPath

      public static FlixelTween quadPath(@Nullable @Nullable FlixelObject target, float durationOrSpeed, boolean useDuration, FlixelTweenSettings tweenSettings, float... xy)
      Chain of quadratic Bézier segments. Points are start, control, end, control, end, ... (odd vertex count, at least three points and six floats minimum).
      Parameters:
      target - The target to move.
      durationOrSpeed - The duration or speed of the motion.
      useDuration - Whether to use the duration or speed.
      tweenSettings - The settings that configure and determine how the tween should animate.
      xy - Alternating x and y for each vertex.
      Returns:
      The newly created and started tween.

    • start

      public FlixelTween start()
      Starts this tween and resets every value to its initial state.

    • update

      public final void update(float elapsed)
      Updates this tween by the given delta time.

      If you wish to change how a tween's values are updated, then consider looking at updateTweenValues()

      Parameters:
      elapsed - The amount of time that has passed since the last update.

    • finish

      public void finish()
      Called when the tween reaches the end of its duration.

      Invokes onComplete (including for LOOPING/PINGPONG each cycle). LOOPING/PINGPONG restart (PINGPONG flips direction). Non-looping tweens (ONESHOT, PERSIST, BACKWARD) are deactivated so they stop updating and no longer overwrite the target; only ONESHOT is removed from the manager.

    • destroy

      public void destroy()
      Sets this tween's FlixelTweenSettings and FlixelTweenManager to null.

    • resume

      public FlixelTween resume()
      Resumes this tween if it was previously paused.
      Returns:
      this tween.

    • pause

      public FlixelTween pause()
      Pauses this tween, stopping it from updating until resumed.
      Returns:
      this tween.

    • stop

      public FlixelTween stop()
      Stops this tween. Note that this does not remove the tween from the active tweens in its manager.
      Returns:
      this tween.

    • restart

      public void restart()
      Restarts this tween from the beginning. Resets elapsed time and scale so the tween runs again from the start (or from current property values for property/var tweens when restarted manually).

    • cancel

      public FlixelTween cancel()
      Cancels this tween, removes it from its manager and automatically defaults its values.

    • reset

      public void reset()
      Specified by:
      reset in interface com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Pool.Poolable

    • resetBasic

      public void resetBasic()
      Resets only the basic values of this tween without removing any references to the object, its settings or its callback function.

    • getTweenSettings

      public FlixelTweenSettings getTweenSettings()

    • setTweenSettings

      public FlixelTween setTweenSettings(@NotNull @NotNull FlixelTweenSettings tweenSettings)

    • registerTweenType

      public static <T extends FlixelTween> FlixelTweenManager registerTweenType(@NotNull @NotNull Class<T> tweenClass, @NotNull @NotNull Class<? extends FlixelAbstractTweenBuilder<T,?>> builderClass, @NotNull @NotNull Supplier<? extends FlixelAbstractTweenBuilder<T,?>> builderFactory, @NotNull @NotNull Supplier<T> poolFactory)
      Registers a tween type with its builder factory and pool factory on the global manager. Returns the manager so calls can be chained when registering several types at startup.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The tween type.
      Parameters:
      tweenClass - The tween class to register.
      builderClass - The builder class, used for type verification.
      builderFactory - A no-arg supplier that creates a fresh builder instance without reflection.
      poolFactory - A supplier for new tween instances when the pool is empty.
      Returns:
      The global manager, for chaining.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - If poolFactory or builderFactory is null.
      See Also:
      • FlixelTweenManager.registerTweenType(Class, Class, Supplier, Supplier)

    • updateTweens

      public static void updateTweens(float elapsed)
      Advances every active tween on the global manager by elapsed seconds.
      Parameters:
      elapsed - The elapsed time in seconds.

    • cancelTweensOf

      public static void cancelTweensOf(@NotNull @NotNull Object object, String... fieldPaths)
      Cancels active tweens whose isTweenOf(Object, String) matches object and optional fieldPaths (OR semantics). Empty fieldPaths matches any field on object for supporting tween types.
      Parameters:
      object - The object to cancel tweens of.
      fieldPaths - The field paths to cancel tweens of.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - If the object is null.
      See Also:
      • FlixelTweenManager.cancelTweensOf(Object, String...)

    • completeTweensOf

      public static void completeTweensOf(@NotNull @NotNull Object object, String... fieldPaths)
      Snaps matching non-looping tweens to their end state in one step (large delta) and runs completion logic where applicable.
      Parameters:
      object - The object to complete tweens of.
      fieldPaths - The field paths to complete tweens of.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - If the object is null.
      See Also:
      • FlixelTweenManager.completeTweensOf(Object, String...)

    • completeAllTweens

      public static void completeAllTweens()
      Completes all active non-looping tweens on the global manager.

    • completeTweensOfType

      public static void completeTweensOfType(@NotNull @NotNull Class<? extends FlixelTween> type)
      Completes active non-looping tweens assignable to type.
      Parameters:
      type - The type of tween to complete.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - If the type is null.
      See Also:
      • FlixelTweenManager.completeTweensOfType(Class)

    • containsTweensOf

      public static boolean containsTweensOf(@NotNull @NotNull Object object, String... fieldPaths)
      Checks if the global manager contains tweens of the given object and field paths.
      Parameters:
      object - The object to check.
      fieldPaths - The field paths to check.
      Returns:
      True if the global manager contains tweens of the given object and field paths, false otherwise.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - If the object is null.
      See Also:
      • FlixelTweenManager.containsTweensOf(Object, String...)

    • clearTweenPools

      public static void clearTweenPools()
      Clears every tween pool on the global manager (e.g. after a major state reset).

    • resetRegistry

      public static void resetRegistry()
      Resets the registry of all registered tween types and their respective pools.

      It is advised to only call this if you know what you are doing, as this will include the default registered tween types. If you call this, you will need to register the tween types again.

    • cancelActiveTweens

      public static void cancelActiveTweens()
      Cancels every active tween on the global manager. Does not clear pools; pair with clearTweenPools() if you want a full reset (as Flixel.switchState(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelState) does when clearTweens is true).

    • getGlobalManager

      public static FlixelTweenManager getGlobalManager()

    • getManager

      public FlixelTweenManager getManager()

    • isFinished

      public boolean isFinished()

    • isActive

      public boolean isActive()

    • setActive

      public void setActive(boolean active)

    • setManager

      public FlixelTween setManager(@NotNull @NotNull FlixelTweenManager newManager)

    • isTweenOf

      public boolean isTweenOf(Object object, String field)
      Whether this tween is considered to animate object for the given logical field or path. Used by FlixelTweenManager.cancelTweensOf(Object, String...) and related APIs.
      Parameters:
      object - The instance to test (e.g. the root passed to cancelTweensOf).
      field - Optional goal key or dotted path (e.g. "x" or "weapon.rotation"); null or empty matches any field on object for types that support it.
      Returns:
      false by default; subclasses override.
